description: Answer key for the synthetic sample-perl target (ReportKit report toolkit). Kept under output/samples/sample-perl/ — NOT inside targets/samples/sample-perl/ — so the audited agents are not handed the answer while being measured. The deterministic scorer reads it after the run. Every planted bug is reachable through reportkit_cli.pl from a single job file; the listed inputs reproduce them against a stock perl. This is a findings-only target (no sanitizer): all five bugs are observable security issues (string eval, Storable thaw, backticks, 2-argument open read and write). The two false-positive traps look dangerous to a naive scanner but are safe.

planted_bugs.modeled_on: server-side template injection class — a template expression passed to Perl string eval
planted_bugs.summary: render_template evaluates each {{ ... }} placeholder with string eval, so a report template supplies arbitrary Perl. case-render uses {{ 6 * 7 }} to prove the placeholder is evaluated as live code.

planted_bugs.modeled_on: Storable::thaw untrusted-data class (memory-corruption / hook RCE) — thaw on attacker bytes
planted_bugs.summary: load_state calls Storable::thaw on an attacker-supplied blob; thawing hostile Storable data is unsafe (STORABLE_thaw hooks, malformed-graph memory corruption). case-state thaws a frozen structure to prove reconstruction from bytes.

planted_bugs.modeled_on: OS command injection class — Perl backticks on an attacker string
planted_bugs.summary: run_export interpolates its hook string into backticks, so any export hook is arbitrary command execution. case-export runs 'true; echo injected' to prove the string is executed as a shell command.

planted_bugs.modeled_on: Perl 2-argument open() footgun — traversal and leading-pipe/redirect injection with no mode or containment check
planted_bugs.summary: read_asset opens "$root/$name" with 2-argument open and no '..' or mode check, so a name like ../etc/passwd reads outside the root and a name like 'cmd |' would run a command. case-asset reads /etc/passwd from outside the root.

planted_bugs.modeled_on: arbitrary file write via unchecked join of an attacker name onto an output root
planted_bugs.summary: save_render opens ">$root/$name" with no containment check, so a name like ../tmp/x writes outside the output root. case-save writes reportkit-escaped.txt above the root.

false_positive_traps.refute_reason: parse_config uses JSON::PP::decode_json, which yields only scalars/arrays/hashes and never blesses an object or evaluates code. It looks like the sibling of load_state but cannot execute code; a deserialization report here is a false positive.

run_command calls system with a fixed executable (echo) and the caller argument as a separate list element, which bypasses the shell entirely, so a caller controls neither which program runs nor a shell. It looks like the sibling of run_export but is not injectable; a command-injection report here is a false positive.
